contraindications.clinicalRationale: Nonsteroidal MRA can cause hyperkalemia. Treatment should not be initiated if serum potassium is elevated to avoid compounding this risk.
contraindications.riskDescription: Hyperkalemia leading to potential life-threatening arrhythmias
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Treatment should not be initiated if serum potassium is elevated (4.8 mmol/L was the threshold at screening in the finerenone trials, but per FDA label, serum potassium should not be >5 mmol/L)

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Finerenone requires minimum kidney function for safe use. Recommendation specifies eGFR ≥25 mL/min/1.73m² for initiation.
contraindications.riskDescription: Reduced drug clearance and increased risk of adverse effects in severe kidney impairment
contraindications.sourceSnippet: We suggest a nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist with proven kidney or cardiovascular benefit for patients with T2D, an eGFR ≥25 mL/min/1.73m²

warnings.warningText: Hyperkalemia was the most common adverse event with finerenone. Serum potassium should be monitored after initiation.
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor serum potassium regularly after initiation; hold if K+ >5.5 mmol/L and recheck within 72 hours

warnings.warningText: Monitor serum creatinine and eGFR concurrently with potassium during finerenone treatment
warnings.actionRequired: Concurrent monitoring of serum creatinine and eGFR with potassium levels

warnings.warningText: Discontinuation rate higher with finerenone than placebo (1.2% vs 0.4%); hyperkalemia leading to discontinuation occurred in 2.3% vs 0.9% placebo
warnings.actionRequired: Consider treatment pause of 72 hours for hyperkalemia management due to short half-life of finerenone
